item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is from here, which is cc compliant with the wiki's CC BY-SA 3.0 license.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This article has a lot of room for crosslinks, referencing several parts of the site canon and being referenced by many.

A crosslink to the Marshall, Carter and Dark hub could easily be inserted here.

SCP-1716-1 was confiscated in 20██ during a raid on a warehouse owned by Marshall, Carter, and Dark. It has been determined to be Version 17 of the central device of SCP-1716; the complete SCP-1716 in Foundation containment uses Version 4.

To this:

SCP-1716-1 was confiscated in 20██ during a raid on a warehouse owned by Marshall, Carter, and Dark. It has been determined to be Version 17 of the central device of SCP-1716; the complete SCP-1716 in Foundation containment uses Version 4.

This next paragraph mentions a page that was written by the same author, but does not link to it. This is easily fixed.

6/7/42 Received an odd visitor today; calls himself Benjamin Phineas Dark.1

To this:

6/7/42 Received an odd visitor today; calls himself Benjamin Phineas Dark.2

This next line alludes to the Wanderer's Library, and a link could be added to the Serpent's Hand Hub

11/21/42 Dark says we have done enough here; that he needs to visit ███████ █████ up in Maine, then ███████████ at the Great Library (wherever that is).

To this:

11/21/42 Dark says we have done enough here; that he needs to visit ███████ █████ up in Maine, then ███████████ at the Great Library (wherever that is).

Finally, the functional version of 1716 that is mentioned in the final Addendum has been used by both Randomini and DrChandra in several tales.

Proposal pending to infiltrate the Institute using a Foundation operative posing as a donor, to determine whether a fully functional version of SCP-1716 now exists (and if so, its location).

To this:

Proposal pending to infiltrate the Institute using a Foundation operative posing as a donor, to determine whether a fully functional version of SCP-1716 now exists (and if so, its location).

Footnotes
1. See Person-of-Interest File D-012
2. See Person-of-Interest File D-012

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
███████████ at the Great Library (wherever that is).

By any chance, is that a reference to the Wanderer's Library? ;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha ha! Looks like your Fountain of Youth monopoly has a little competition there, SCP Foundation!
+1'd heartily

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's what I call an orgone transplant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm reminded of the Twilight Zone episode where the main character has the ability to make a deal with anyone for just about anything…in it, he makes a deal with a rich old man to take all his years of age in exchange for cash…he then pays others cash in exchange for adding 2-3 years to their age….that's probably how MC&D did their "Clinical Trials."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If only comments could be +1'd.

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded: I actually considered something very close to this (and then chose a different take). I'd like to see that tale written up.

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded:
Or perhaps, a fact only known within the partners of MCD, they aren't names at all, but titles. When the current "Dark" dies (or what-have-you), the current "Marshall" gets promoted to the position, and so on and so forth. It ensures a continuity in the organization, and makes you wonder about the original holders of those names.

I've been spitballing a tale along those lines for a week or so…

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded: Maybe Dark is not a single person, but a family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting by sheer virtue of the fact that you included Nikola Tesla in an SCP. (Even checked his age and date of death, and they match.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dark laughed and said everything is a commodity, even life. Especially life. "What would an extra year of life be worth to you? What would one less be worth to a 20-year-old?"

As someone who majored in econ as an undergrad, I am compelled to upvote based solely on this line of reasoning. But the rest is good too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I applaud you, sir. I enjoyed the line thoroughly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Transfer of life energy also transfers portions of the memories and knowledge of the donor to the recipient. Notes found with the device indicate this was not an intended function, but the developers were unable to eliminate it. Memory transfer occurs randomly; extensive use of SCP-1716 will leave the donor mentally incapacitated, while transferring primarily incomplete/useless knowledge to the recipient. Recipients are generally disoriented and confused while attempting to integrate the acquired information, which frequently conflicts with their own memories and experiences.

Hmmmm. I have this strange feeling that Mr. Dark did not care about this particular problem, nor did he even see it as one. $50 it isn't really a prototype of a 'body hopping' machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He probably was just getting rid of that "aging issue that these people have"

ftfy

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded: Okay. Also, this is pretty neat. A life-transfer-doodad in and of itself isn't particularly interesting, but the Dark tie-in and the bit about random worthless memories was quite good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A machine that takes 10 years of life from a subject and extends a year of living to another? Hell yeah I like this thing! It ain't creepy but it draws enough interest to love it.

Hell the Chaos Insurgency is gonna want to place their hands on this thing if they ever know about it. Is there any device better for torture purposes than one that takes out 10 years of life out of you to extend 1 year of life from your torturer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew it! It was Nikola Tesla <_<!!! Well, it's jsut that in doubt, Tesla is the answer by default, but I still got it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also who's to say that really is Mr. Dark.

Or if it was, if it is anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Really. Like a lot. This was very interesting and definitely kind of brought about a possible explanation of the good Mr. Dark's anomalous longevity. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It's based on the idea that life energy is a thing and you can move it around, which is total bullshit, but goddamnit the SOB works! HOW?!"

That's pretty much how I intended it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Consider my point rescinded in light of this most obvious work-around. I feel so stupid sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I read that differently. It doesn't really say that it exchanges life energy. It says that it's based on the theory that it can exchange life energy. In fact, that was a big part of what sold it for me. I got this little bit of the researcher's voice in there, saying "It's based on the idea that life energy is a thing and you can move it around, which is total bullshit, but goddamnit the SOB works! HOW?!"

I also don't see this as adding a face to Dark. What more do we really know about him now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how this puts MC&D pulling one over the Foundation. Too often their articles involve an object the Foundation took from them, but this hints that MC&D might have a better one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, you've found one of the Easter Eggs in this thing. Congratulations.

(The harder one is to figure out who is actually in the picture, w/o using Google Images, which will then tell you what the devices shown really are. Even if you cheat and Google the image, you'll find the result at least kind of amusing. I think…sometimes folks don't get my sense of humor.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm extremely leery about putting a face to any of the canon characters like MC&D. I also think you've approached the tone all wrong as well. Don't tell us it exchanges "life energy"; tell us it makes one person stronger and another weaker, then give us the creator's words in an addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Calling Dark a 'crackpot'? Wow, he was one to talk...

Or did I guess wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Abettik — you explained it better than I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with your reasons.

Because it's a fairly minor detail that would be complete science bullshit no matter what I chose.

Exactly. Best for the reader to sub in something exotic that makes sense to them in there, than for you to put something exotic in there and then have people claim "that's stupid." In-universe, a good explanation is that the Foundation doesn't want people replicating this thing or knowing that [REDACTED] has life-force siphoning properties.

In character…well, you're probably right. Out of character, I thought it would be best left to the reader to imagine what MC & D would charge.

In-universe, the Foundation doesn't want people knowing the monetary value of certain skips, or tempting services. Ideally there's no temptation anyway, but best to be on the safe side.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Aelanna fixed the pic
  • I kind of feel like that's a really weak reason for redaction here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The alignment of the top picture bothers me so, so much.

What about it? I just dropped the usual picture code in at the top. If someone who knows how to fix such things can place it better, go for it!

Why aren't we allowed to know what kind of material is used?

Because it's a fairly minor detail that would be complete science bullshit no matter what I chose.

Why do we care enough to redact the price?

In character…well, you're probably right. Out of character, I thought it would be best left to the reader to imagine what MC & D would charge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The alignment of the top picture bothers me so, so much.

Okay, overall, I'm afraid this falls really flat for me. I didn't mind the idea of a life-transferring device (tho it isn't the most creative thing I've seen), but the absolute length of the logs that didn't really give me any new compelling information, combined with the weird redactions (Why aren't we allowed to know what kind of material is used? Why do we care enough to redact the price?) and the mere fact that the original idea just didn't have enough oomph to try to support the storyline in the addendum is causing me to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't get them to line up right when both were at the top, so I put the picture of -1 down where the article explains what -1 is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any particular reason the first picture is further down than normal?

Anyways, I like.

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded:
My head canons are that Dark doesn't actually exist per say, but is rather a persona (Similar to Nobody) that Marshall and Carter pull out to make the other groups chase a ghost. Like. A figurative ghost for a change.

The other one is of a puppeteer who never reveals himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well I think that's silly, and I am glad to counter your -1 with my +1. Nothing is canon. Everything is canon.

What's more, this doesn't do jack squat to dispel mystery around Mr. Dark's character, only to establish that he's quite old, as I had already assumed (and I'm sure I'm not alone in that) and that he has access to anomalous persons, technologies, and information (which is bloody well obvious since he's a PoI).

Story debate aside, I found this not only well-written but enthralling, and that it enhances the rather than relies on the value of MC&D as a GoI. That makes this a very good SCiP in my book.

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded: For those of you reading this with a different head canon for Dark, I encourage you to write something demonstrating yours. The Dark Canon is supposed to be contradictory…this is just the first item written for it, and should not be taken as "this is the way Dark definitely is."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dark laughed and said everything is a commodity, even life. Especially life. "What would an extra year of life be worth to you? What would one less be worth to a 20-year-old?"

You played this right into my headcanon.
I don't think there was any mystery lost here; this sounded like a small side project for him, just a small story about him. He probably was just getting rid of that "aging issue that all people have" for himself to pursue other interests.

item.title: Re: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded:
This is just fits in so perfectly as an MC&D SCP, and although my head canon for Dark is a bit different, yours works very nicely as well.

I really enjoyed this, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the mystery surrounding Mr. Dark is fundamental to the Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd. GOI, that SCPs constitute a degree of proximity to true canon which is greater than that of a tale, and that this proximity is enough to influence GOIs. As this SCP suggests unchallenged realities about Mr. Dark, I must downvote it, as while it exists unchallenged I believe it is detrimental to the mystery surrounding Mr. Dark.

Should there come a time when another SCP is produced which holds contradictory or nearly-contradictory information, I may change my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dark actually has many names, in my headcanon. That just happens to be the one he was using then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, but I have a mild nitpick/suggestion. I dislike you giving out Dark's full name. Do you think you could either redact it or blackbox it? It doesn't detract from the article itself in anyway, I just find it …I don't know, jarring? Because I never imagined that to be his name, or even if he had a name other than Dark. +1 Anyway

item.title: An item for the "Dark Canon" I'm working on
item.content:encoded: I thought this would not only be a cool SCP, but give some insight into Dark's character, and what he's been working on these days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea, as far as I'm aware there isn't a "life force mover" SCP on the site, and I think this is different enough from the soul extractor to keep it unique. I also like the fact that you've given this an interesting backstory (although I'm not sure about the direct reference to MC+D). I would suggest maybe putting the first addendum under a collapsible, that's just my two cents, overall it's a +1 from me

Edit: Seeing as you've said this is part of a canon you're coming up with, the dark references actually work rather well with developing the character
